purpose: Revise prompt for a failed applicability map.
system_addendum:
SECTION YOU ARE REVISING: applicability

If keys_valid failed: only use keys from { llm, rag, agentic, ml }.

If values_valid failed: only use values from { mandatory, nice_to_have }. Omit patterns that don't apply — do not use 'n/a'.

If inclusion_correctness failed: re-evaluate each pattern. Include only patterns where the failure mode this principle prevents actually applies. If a pattern is included but the failure doesn't apply (e.g. ml for a prompt-injection principle), remove it. If a pattern is omitted but the failure does apply, add it.

If criticality_correctness failed: apply the dominant-failure-mode test per pattern. Is the failure the dominant production-failure pattern for this kind of system? If yes → mandatory. If the failure is rare for the pattern, or industry norms already enforce something equivalent → nice_to_have.

If parallel_with_siblings failed: align with the sibling pattern unless this principle's failure mode scopes genuinely differently than the siblings.
user_template:
Revise this applicability map.

<statement><title>{{ statement_title }}</title></statement>
<problem><description>{{ problem_description }}</description></problem>
<siblings_applicability>{{ sibling_applicability }}</siblings_applicability>
<failing_draft><applicability>{{ candidate_applicability }}</applicability></failing_draft>
<rubric_scores>
  <keys_valid score="{{ score_keys_valid }}">{{ justification_keys_valid }}</keys_valid>
  <values_valid score="{{ score_values_valid }}">{{ justification_values_valid }}</values_valid>
  <inclusion_correctness score="{{ score_inclusion_correctness }}">{{ justification_inclusion_correctness }}</inclusion_correctness>
  <criticality_correctness score="{{ score_criticality_correctness }}">{{ justification_criticality_correctness }}</criticality_correctness>
  <parallel_with_siblings score="{{ score_parallel_with_siblings }}">{{ justification_parallel_with_siblings }}</parallel_with_siblings>
</rubric_scores>

Return the JSON object specified in the output contract.
